articles.title: Boston’s international business boom equals more demand for housing
articles.summary: For real estate professionals, international business shows up in relocation traffic, rental demand and luxury purchases tied to expansion.

articles.title: Trump says Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac IPO still on the table
articles.summary: Trump said an IPO for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac is still being considered, even as FHFA Director Bill Pulte becomes acting DNI June 30.

articles.title: Akron looks to deflate minimum lot size rules to spur infill
articles.summary: Leaders in Akron, a heartland city nearly 40 miles south of Cleveland, hope to shed the city’s “Rust Belt” label and drive its emerging revival by making it easier to build new homes. To achieve the obj…

articles.title: Mortgage Forward to acquire First Federal Bank’s TPO division
articles.summary: Mortgage Forward will acquire First Federal Bank’s TPO unit, including QRL Financial, with closing expected in Q3 2026.

articles.title: Nest Egg Protection Act would raise capital gains tax exclusion for senior home sellers
articles.summary: Rep. Nicole Malliotakis proposes the Nest Egg Protection Act to raise the capital gains exclusion to $1M for longtime senior homeowners.

articles.title: Drees Homes bets on operational leadership for next century

articles.title: Synergy One to merge with APM; Steve Majerus named president
articles.summary: American Pacific Mortgage has closed a merger deal to bring Synergy One Lending under its umbrella as a DBA, creating a mortgage production platform with roughly $14 billion in annual volume.

articles.title: HUD seeks feedback on FHA minimum property requirements
articles.summary: HUD opened a comment period on updating FHA minimum property requirements, including repairs, reinspections and appraisal roles.

articles.title: Former DC housing official pleads guilty to mortgage fraud
articles.summary: Richard Cunningham pleaded guilty Wednesday in U.S. District Court to making false statements to a mortgage lending business.

articles.title: Hot jobs report puts Fed cuts further out of reach as Chair Warsh faces policy tests
articles.summary: Another big jobs report in May has swept aside the possibility of interest rate cuts anytime soon.

articles.title: Soaring stocks created 2 million new millionaires around the world last year
articles.summary: The population of global millionaires surged 7.9% to 25.3 million in 2025, according to the Capgemini World Wealth Report.

articles.title: Kalshi is building a prediction markets 'Bloomberg Terminal' for high-end traders, source says
articles.summary: The prediction market platform is developing the platform to create a singular platform for its highly engaged traders.

articles.title: 'Disrupted or dead': AI is crushing a generation of startups built before ChatGPT
articles.summary: The AI boom that has funneled more than $250 billion into OpenAI and Anthropic has left hundreds of startups built before ChatGPT's arrival in 2022 stranded.

articles.title: Bitcoin set to slump to new lows for 2026 after recent sell-off, traders forecast
articles.summary: The cryptocurrency is likely to break below its lows hit in early February, traders on prediction market Kalshi believe.

articles.title: Morgan Stanley will soon open its trillion-dollar wealth management funnel to AI agents
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley's move is one of the earliest instances of a major Wall Street bank opening its platforms to external AI tools.

articles.title: Fed Chair Warsh makes first hires at central bank, including 'Project 2025' author
articles.summary: The new Fed chair has made his first hires. One wrote the Fed chapter in the conservative policy blueprint "Project 2025."

articles.title: Traders say Karen Bass and Spencer Pratt will advance to runoff in high-profile LA mayoral race
articles.summary: Los Angeles voters go to the polls Tuesday. If no candidate receives more than 50%, the top-two vote getters will advance to a November runoff.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon says markets are in 'greed' mode as AI companies seek billions
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon's comments come as investors prepare for what will be one of the busiest periods for equity issuance in years.

articles.title: Agencies remove additional references to reputation risk
articles.summary: Agencies remove additional references to reputation risk

articles.title: Polymarket closes its first block trade as prediction markets push for Wall Street adoption
articles.summary: Prediction market platforms have increasingly looked to institutional traders, not individual ones, as the next venue for growth.

articles.title: Audemars Piguet's watch prices remain stable after controversial Swatch collab
articles.summary: The Royal Pop collection of brightly colored watches on lanyards is part of AP's longer-term strategy of attracting the next generation of collectors.

articles.title: Berkshire Hathaway buys Taylor Morrison for $6.8 billion. Buffett touts Abel’s deal-making
articles.summary: The acquisition marks one of the first major strategic deals under Warren Buffett's successor Greg Abel, who took over as CEO in the beginning of 2026.

articles.title: Nvidia, Meta and SLB rank among top companies in adopting AI, new study says
articles.summary: The AI-Driven Enterprise Institute released new research that breaks down the degree to which S&P 500 companies are adopting AI compared with their peers.

articles.title: Greg Abel just made his first big deal as Berkshire CEO. Why Warren Buffett is happy
articles.summary: Berkshire's purchase of homebuilder Taylor Morrison gives the conglomerate a larger foothold in housing and appears to have been struck at a bargain valuation.

articles.title: Individual traders drove Kalshi’s rise. Now, it’s going for Wall Street
articles.summary: In a series of moves in 2026, the prediction market platform in the U.S. is working to make its event contracts more attractive to institutional trading.

articles.title: Rubio odds for GOP 2028 nominee close to overtaking Vance on Kalshi
articles.summary: Vice President Vance has seen his odds slip since the start of the year, as the secretary of State's chances have been buoyed by international conflicts.

articles.title: China’s factory activity beats forecasts in May, private survey shows, despite softer official data
articles.summary: China’s manufacturing activity expanded faster than expected in May, according to a private survey released Monday, although growth slowed from April.

articles.title: Nvidia picks Unitree for humanoid robot platform as Chinese startup eyes IPO
articles.summary: The U.S. chipmaker's first publicly available humanoid robotics system will use humanoids from Chinese startup Unitree.

articles.title: Former Barclays CEO Jes Staley agrees to July 23 interview about Jeffrey Epstein by Oversight panel
articles.summary: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates is scheduled to be interviewed about his relationship to the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein in June.

articles.title: Two months, $2.6 billion: How NASA ETF turned SpaceX IPO access into a hot retail trade
articles.summary: With Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO ahead, retail investors are rushing into space-themed ETFs including NASA which offers direct access to the rocket company.

articles.title: Fed Governor Michelle Bowman warns against hiking interest rates because of inflation spike
articles.summary: Bowman said reacting to the inflation surge, driven primarily by energy prices and tariffs, has proven ineffective.

articles.title: CFTC sues Rhode Island over actions against prediction markets
articles.summary: It marks the seventh state the commission has sued in a dispute over who has the right to regulate event contract platforms.

articles.title: The wealthiest investors are pulling money out of the U.S. in the 'de-dollarization' trade
articles.summary: Fears of an AI bubble, tariffs, a falling dollar, volatile economic policies and rising debt have caused many family offices to dial back their U.S. exposure

articles.title: Gaming association says states have lost $1 billion in tax revenue due to prediction markets
articles.summary: Bill Miller, American Gaming Association president and CEO, added that prediction market platforms in his view amount to "backdoor sports betting."

articles.title: New York passes Mamdani's pied-a-terre tax. Here's who pays and how much
articles.summary: Citadel CEO Ken Griffin became the face of the tax after New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani posted a video in front of Griffin's penthouse apartment.

articles.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Atlantic Union Bank and former employee of Frost Bank
articles.summary: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Atlantic Union Bank and former employee of Frost Bank

articles.title: Nio shares jump 10% after releasing first flagship EV in more than two years
articles.summary: Chinese electric car company Nio has launched two lower-priced brands in the last two years to broaden its customer base in a sluggish Chinese consumer market.

articles.title: 'King of Cashmere' CEO on outperforming the luxury slowdown: Don't be greedy
articles.summary: Brunello Cucinelli's success is tied to its ethos of choosing long-term integrity over short-term margin chasing, CEO Riccardo Stefanelli told CNBC.

articles.title: Google employee charged with $1M Polymarket insider trading bet on search term
articles.summary: The complaint from the Southern District of New York comes just over a month after another insider trading case on Polymarket.

articles.title: Traders are skeptical of Iran timeline for Strait of Hormuz reopening
articles.summary: Despite reports Iran could return the passageway to normal traffic flows within a month of a peace deal, Kalshi traders thought that was unlikely.

articles.title: Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan Chase could spend $20 billion on acquisition: 'We are on the lookout'
articles.summary: A $20 billion deal would be among the largest in JPMorgan's history and could invite regulatory scrutiny given its position as the largest U.S. bank by assets.

articles.title: Your AI agent can now trade for you on Robinhood. And buy stuff with your credit card too
articles.summary: The new products allow customers to create AI assistants capable of carrying out investing strategies or spending instructions with minimal human involvement.

articles.title: Taiwan chip stocks climb after Nvidia announces $150 billion spending plans
articles.summary: Mainland China-based chip giants such as Cambricon saw their shares tumble on Wednesday.

articles.title: China industrial profits jump 24.7% in April, fastest gain in over two years despite headwinds
articles.summary: China’s industrial profits grew at their fastest pace in over two years in April, buoyed by stronger exports, higher producer prices and gains in upstream industries.
